title: Nephrodium biseriale
intro: Nephrodium biseriale, also known as Maidenhair Fern, is a type of vascular plant belonging to the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can aid in wound healing and reduce the risk of infection. The bioactive constituents of Nephrodium biseriale include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Nephrodium biseriale include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Nephrodium biseriale belongs to the division Pteridophyta, which is a group of vascular plants that reproduce via spores. Within the division, it is classified as a member of the class Polypodiopsida. This class is further divided into the order Polypodiales, which includes a diverse range of ferns and their allies. Nephrodium biseriale specifically falls under the family Dryopteridaceae, a family of shield ferns. It is also known as a member of the genus Blechnum, which is a group of ferns with a global distribution.
